rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we didn't meet our endpoint, but we actually had a great response
Speaker said: the drug's relatively toxic, so one of the things was we had to take patients off because of the toxicity of the drug

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Reliance on MAPK pathway inhibition without addressing upstream TAZ-CAMTA1 driver
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: about 20 patients actually responded really well
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: The drug's relatively toxic, so one of the things was we had to take patients off because of the toxicity

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Requires millimolar concentrations, not drug-like potency
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: It's a tool compound active at millimolar concentrations, not suitable for clinic

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: TRAMETINIB inhibition of MAPK pathway in TAZ-CAMTA1 driven EHE
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Non‑randomized, open‑label single‑arm design without biomarker stratification beyond EHE diagnosis
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No selection for CDK9 or TEAD pathway activation status
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Underpowered to meet primary endpoint; small sample size

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Current tool compounds (e.g., MGH CP1) show only millimolar potency and lack drug‑like properties
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on TEAD inhibition as monotherapy despite low potency and need for combination

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Broad CDK inhibition with off‑target effects; not a selective CDK9 probe for TAZ‑CAMTA1 context
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Use of non‑selective CDK inhibitors may cause toxicity limiting dose escalation

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that CDK9 inhibition caused strong apoptosis in EHE cell lines, then concluded that CDK9 inhibitors are ready for clinical development despite acknowledging toxicity and lack of selectivity

key_data_gaps: No biomarker‑stratified analysis beyond EHE diagnosis
Lack of comparative arm to standard of care

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify patients with high CDK9 activity or TEAD dependency to enrich for responders to CDK9 or TEAD‑targeted combos
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that CDK9 appears to be the target of dinaciclib and that CDK9 inhibition blows apart the transcriptional complex
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate transcriptomic and phosphoproteomic data to define a CDK9‑activity signature for patient selection

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine low‑dose trametinib with a potent TEAD inhibitor to achieve synergistic suppression of TAZ‑CAMTA1 signaling while mitigating toxicity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated synergistic effect of trametinib with a TEAD inhibitor in cell lines
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model drug‑drug interaction networks to prioritize TEAD inhibitors with favorable pharmacodynamics for combination

crispro_opportunity.description: Redevelop the TEAD fragment‑based NMR hits into higher‑affinity, drug‑like molecules using CrisPRO’s structure‑guided design pipeline
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker reported progress from 10 µM to 500 nM affinity but still needs another log reduction
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict optimal scaffold modifications to improve potency and drug‑likeness while preserving TEAD interface binding

cited_competitors.name: MGH CP1 (TEAD inhibitor)
cited_competitors.context: Tool compound used in preclinical synergy studies with trametinib

cited_competitors.name: Dinaciclib (CDK inhibitor)
cited_competitors.context: Showed in vivo tumor reduction in xenograft models

unresolved_questions: What is the precise role of the CAMTA1 portion of the TAZ‑CAMTA1 fusion in driving oncogenesis?
Can more selective CDK9 inhibitors be developed that retain efficacy without off‑target toxicity?
